The '''comparative''' degree in Aelaki is formed through morphological '''reduplication of C₂''' (the second consonant of the root). It applies to [[Adjective|adjectives]], [[Adverb|adverbs]], and numerals.

== Formation ==

The comparative is created by reduplicating the second root consonant (C₂) within the templatic structure of the word.

== Examples ==

{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Base Form !! Comparative !! Part of Speech
|-
| ''koran'' "bright" || ''kororan'' "brighter" || [[Adjective]]
|-
| ''zadate'' "lengthy" || ''zadadate'' "lengthier" || [[Adverb]]
|}

== Related Degrees ==

* '''[[Positive]]''': Unmarked base form
* '''[[Superlative]]''': Formed by umlaut (vowel fronting) rather than reduplication
* '''[[Negative]]''': Formed by /f/-infix

[[Category:adjective things]]
[[Category:git synced pages]]
